our_argument_summary: EXP-253 wholesale-rewrote §4.2.2 (committed via decider integration) to lead with the dome's published misstatement of the standard-physics eclipse prediction (Domain-11 falsification rule + countdown banner both treat 0 nT as the "globe prediction"), preserves the existing Chapman 1933 paragraph as supporting prose, and closes with a "BOU 2017 already falsifies by the dome's own ±3 nT rule" hammer paragraph linking to §4.2.5. Decider applied four c1 fixes during integration (H1 explicit-arithmetic phrasing, H2 strawman→treated as zero, H3 universality→criterion+banner scoping, H4 §4.2.3 semicolon→period). Forward-link to §4.2.7 (id=p4-427) added as parenthetical second-failure flag. Verdict (Refuted by Data) holds; this c2 attacks the integrated text with attention to surviving holes the c1 review couldn't see post-integration.

live_quote_domain_11_falsification_rule.live_form: Eclipse 2026 — Fails if measured anomaly is within ±3 nT of 0 (globe prediction).

live_quote_countdown_banner.live_form_template_literal: 🌒 ' + eclipseDays + ' days until Aug 12, 2026 eclipse — ECM predicts -' + predVal + ' nT, globe predicts 0 nT. Requires Kp < 2.

c1_fixes_applied_correctly.H1_arithmetic: applied — closing paragraph reads "3.6× the falsification half-width (|−10.9| / 3 ≈ 3.6), well outside the dome's own ±3 nT acceptance window"
c1_fixes_applied_correctly.H2_strawman_softened: applied — "replaced with a strawman" → "treated as zero in the dome's published rule"
c1_fixes_applied_correctly.H3_scope_tightened: applied — "The dome treats zero..." → "The dome's published falsification criterion and live countdown banner treat zero..."

code_analysis_tags_note: SEC-4.2.2 is prose, not a WIN entry — code_analysis_tags do not apply. The dome-code aspects of the eclipse argument live in WIN-010, not in this section.

holes_found.description: The paragraph "The dome's site separately characterizes the globe prediction as having 'no physical mechanism' for eclipse magnetic effects" mischaracterizes the dome's actual published position. The dome's Tier 1 magnetic prediction (predictions.html, live and current) explicitly says: "Globe prediction: 0.0 nT systematic (or ionospheric Sq disruption — competing mechanism exists, see WIN-068 honest caveat)." The dome's site DOES acknowledge the Sq mechanism exists for eclipse magnetic effects. The "no mechanism" framing on the dome's own site applies to the GRAVITY tiers (Tier 2 LaCoste-Romberg "ionospheric mechanism cannot produce ground-level g changes" and Tier 3 SG "no mechanism"), not to the magnetic Tier 1. By attributing a "no physical mechanism" stance to the dome on the magnetic test, §4.2.2 hands a sophisticated dome defender a clean rebuttal: "we explicitly cite Sq in our published predictions; you're strawmanning our position." This paragraph was inherited from the prior version of §4.2.2 — EXP-253 preserved it verbatim — and slipped past the c1 review because it was treated as already-vetted prose. With a wholesale rewrite cycle now committed, the inherited misattribution is operating in a freshly-attended section and should be corrected.
holes_found.recommendation: Revise the paragraph to acknowledge the WIN-068 honest_caveat and pivot to the structural argument. Suggested replacement: "The dome's predictions.html does cite the Sq mechanism — 'Globe prediction: 0.0 nT systematic (or ionospheric Sq disruption — competing mechanism exists, see WIN-068 honest caveat)' — but the operational Tier 1 falsification rule, as published, still tests measured anomaly against 0 nT, not against a pre-registered Sq-subtracted residual. Acknowledging Sq in a footnote and then writing a falsification rule that ignores it leaves the test discriminating only against the strawman 0 nT, not against Chapman's 5–25 nT." This preserves the §4.2.2 critique while removing the misattribution and pre-empting the defender's easiest retreat.
evidence_sources: raw-text/03-predictions.txt lines 22-45 — dome's published predictions registry

Search of dome raw-text + live.html: phrase 'no physical mechanism' does not appear anywhere in dome material; closest dome-side phrasings are 'no mechanism' (gravity Tier 3) and 'cannot produce ground-level g changes' (gravity Tier 2)

holes_found.description: The closing trilemma is missing a fourth horn that a sophisticated defender will reach for: "the rule applies to the post-Sq residual, not the raw measurement; the WIN-068 honest_caveat is the disclosure that the test is about residual ECM-class anomaly after Sq is subtracted out." Under that reading, BOU 2017's −10.9 nT (a raw measurement) is not directly comparable to the rule's ±3 nT envelope (a residual specification), and the trilemma's first horn loses its bite. The closing paragraph acknowledges "the dome's eclipse formula uses a 2017 Boulder (BOU) observation of −10.9 nT as one of its baselines" but does not engage with the post-Sq-residual defense. The c1 review's bou_already_falsifies_overreach_test addressed the related '2017 was different conditions' defense, but did not cover the post-Sq-residual reading.
holes_found.recommendation: Expand the trilemma to a four-way collapse, or add a parenthetical addressing the residual reading. Suggested: "A defender might respond that the ±3 nT rule applies to the post-Sq residual after Chapman is subtracted, not to the raw measurement; in that case, the dome owes a published, pre-registered Sq-subtraction methodology for the August 2026 measurement (so the test's pass/fail outcome is not chosen after the data lands). No such methodology is published. Without it, 'the rule means residual' becomes a moveable goalpost." This neutralizes the residual defense without re-opening the body of the section.
evidence_sources: https://john09289.github.io/predictions/live.html Domain 11 falsification rule (does not specify 'measured raw' vs 'measured residual' — ambiguity is exploitable)

holes_found.description: TLDR third clause — "and one of the dome's own baseline inputs already sits outside its ±3 nT 'globe' window" — strips the trilemma framing and asserts the BOU-2017-already-falsifies argument as a stand-alone fact. A reader who only reads the TLDR sees it as a domain-of-applicability claim. The body's closing paragraph properly frames it as a trilemma the dome must navigate; the TLDR doesn't. Defender-pickable: "the rule applies to the 2026 measurement, not the 2017 baseline — your TLDR commits a category error." The body's closing paragraph rescues the framing, but TLDR-only readers (the design intent of progressive disclosure) miss the rescue.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional tightening — replace TLDR third clause with "...and the dome's published rule, applied consistently, also flags the dome's own −10.9 nT BOU-2017 baseline input." Phrasing avoids the bare "sits outside" assertion and signals that the argument depends on the rule's domain of applicability — a question the body addresses.

holes_found.description: The c1 stronger_arguments[0] (E001-E009 dome-published predictions all sit outside ±3 nT, providing a tighter form of second-horn collapse) was not incorporated during integration. This is not a hole — it's a missed strengthening — but the recommendation stands: a single sentence added near the closing paragraph would tighten the section by showing that the dome's own 2026 predicted values would themselves cross the ±3 nT rule from the dome's side, making it impossible for the dome to read its own forecasts as discriminating against a 0-nT globe.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional. If integration appetite remains: add one sentence near the closing trilemma — "And the dome's own published 2026 predictions (E001-E009: BOU baseline −5 to −13 nT, W004 baseline −10 to −26 nT) all sit outside its ±3 nT window from the same direction as BOU 2017, so even on a 2026-only reading of the rule, the dome's own forecast guarantees the rule will fire if the forecast is right."

holes_found.description: The new parenthetical "(A second, independent failure appears in §4.2.7: the dome's own published parameters produce a totality zone roughly 1.3 km wide — about 1/220 the observed path of totality — via an argument that does not depend on the misstatement established here.)" was added during integration to forward-link to §4.2.7. The forward-link resolves (id=p4-427 verified present) and the cited numbers (1.3 km / 1/220) match §4.2.7's body. However, embedding a second major argument inside the closing paragraph dilutes the BOU-2017 hammer's rhetorical impact. A reader sees "by the dome's own criterion, the dome has already failed" and immediately gets routed to a different (geometric) failure. Both arguments are correct but they compete for the closing emphasis.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional structural tweak. Either (a) hoist the §4.2.7 forward-link to a separate one-sentence paragraph after the BOU-2017 hammer, so each argument owns its closing beat; or (b) leave as-is, accepting that double-stacking failures is a stylistic choice that rewards careful readers. Do not block on this — the parenthetical is technically clean.

stronger_arguments.description: Engaging the WIN-068 honest_caveat directly turns a strawman-shaped paragraph ("dome attributes no mechanism to globe") into a structural critique ("dome cites Chapman in a footnote, then writes the test as if Chapman doesn't exist"). The structural critique is harder to rebut and aligns with the section's existing misstatement framing.

If the operator wants to publish a quantitative version of the trilemma, the residual-vs-raw ambiguity in the Domain-11 rule is a publishable instance of the broader 'unresolved methodology before measurement' critique used elsewhere in §4.2.x. A short bullet list — (a) raw measurement + 0-nT comparator → strawman of Chapman; (b) post-Sq residual + 0-nT comparator → owes pre-registered Sq methodology; (c) 2026-only domain + 0-nT comparator → dome's own 2026 forecasts trigger the rule, non-discriminating; (d) ignore BOU 2017's 10.9 nT raw measurement → concede globe-physics datum is being used as input — converts the prose trilemma into a foursquare table the dome must answer.

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-253's core observation — that the Domain-11 falsification rule and countdown banner both register "globe predicts 0 nT" — is real, current, verified live, and operationally load-bearing in the dome's own published methodology. The rule is the artifact the dome publishes as its discrimination test, not a stray remark, and it does treat 0 nT as the standard-physics expectation in the operational rule. The new lead correctly identifies this as the argumentative center of gravity for §4.2.2.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The misstatement framing is sound; the strawman-paragraph carryover and the missing fourth-horn defense are corrigible without dismantling the section. With those holes patched, the rewrite achieves what EXP-253 was commissioned to do — make the load-bearing dome error the lead, not the post-Chapman afterthought.

recommended_action_detail: The section's structural critique is sound and the verdict (Refuted by Data) holds. Two corrigible holes (one major: 'no physical mechanism' misattribution; one moderate: missing post-Sq-residual fourth horn) deserve a small follow-up patch. The minor-severity items (TLDR phrasing, c1 stronger_arguments[0] not incorporated, parenthetical placement) are polish and not blocking. Suggest the decider file ISS-followups for the major and moderate items, queue a tight EXP for the misstatement-paragraph rewrite (single-paragraph patch, ~80 words), and absorb the moderate item as a parenthetical or footnote in the same patch.

text_fingerprint.verdict_chip_target: Refuted by Data (eclipse argument cluster — WIN-010)

deeper_analysis: The integrated §4.2.2 makes a strong argumentative move (lead with the dome's own falsification rule + banner, demote Chapman to support, close with the BOU-2017 trilemma) and the four c1 fixes have visibly tightened the prose. The defender's strongest extant retreat is no longer the body's framing — it is the inherited 'no physical mechanism' paragraph that contradicts the dome's own predictions.html Tier 1 disclosure. That paragraph is a single sentence in the body but it's the only place a careful dome reader can land a clean rebuttal without conceding the trilemma. The closing trilemma's missing fourth horn (post-Sq residual reading of the rule) is the second-best retreat; covering it is one parenthetical sentence. Both are tractable in a single follow-up EXP. Verdict-level disposition (Refuted by Data) does not change. The WIN-010 detail evidence and §4.2.5 input-chain framing remain consistent with this section's rewrite; no cross-page churn implied.

citations_failed: 'no physical mechanism' attributed to dome — not present in dome raw-text or live.html for the magnetic test. The dome's actual phrasing on magnetic Tier 1 is '0.0 nT systematic (or ionospheric Sq disruption — competing mechanism exists, see WIN-068 honest caveat)'. The 'no mechanism' / 'cannot produce' phrasings on the dome's site apply to the gravity Tier 2/3 (LaCoste/SG), not magnetic. See major hole for recommended remediation.

summary_for_decider: Verdict holds. Text quality after EXP-253 + c1 fixes is good. Two holes worth a follow-up patch: (1) MAJOR — 'no physical mechanism' paragraph mischaracterizes the dome's actual position (the dome's predictions.html Tier 1 explicitly cites Sq disruption + WIN-068 honest_caveat); (2) MODERATE — closing trilemma is missing a post-Sq-residual fourth horn. Both are paragraph-level edits, ~80-150 words combined. Three minor items are polish-grade. Recommend a single tight EXP for items (1) + (2), no need to re-open the lead/body structure.
